principles.name: Structure Before Implementation
principles.description: Design before code. Understand the problem, define the shape, then build.

principles.name: Version Everything
principles.description: Version, date, owner, and assumptions on all changes.

principles.name: Stable IDs Over Strings
principles.description: Use immutable identifiers for references, not display names.

principles.name: Explicit Lifecycle States
principles.description: Every artifact has a state: active | deprecated | replaced.

principles.name: Breaking Changes Require Gates
principles.description: Version bump + migration plan + rollback plan.

principles.name: Feature-Flag Risky Rollouts
principles.description: Gradual exposure for changes with uncertain impact.

principles.name: Bias Toward Simplicity
principles.description: Minimal viable structure. Don't build what you don't need yet.

principles.name: Filesystem-Native Offline Default
principles.description: QB bundles are filesystem-native. ZIP is archive only, not primary transport.

principles.name: Claude Memory Is Session Context
principles.description: Claude reads MEMORY.md at session start. Keep it current and concise.

principles.name: Cross-Project Dispatch Via QB
principles.description: All cross-project communication uses QB INBOX/OUTBOX filesystem handoff.

principles.name: TroyOS Is Portfolio Authority
principles.description: TroyOS QB is canonical for portfolio state. All project QBs report here.

principles.name: Queue Before Execute
principles.description: All non-autonomous work goes to WORK_QUEUE before execution. Autonomous actions (read, log, scaffold) run immediately. See AUTONOMY_MATRIX for boundary.

principles.name: Priority Order: Blocking > High > Med > Low
principles.description: Within same priority, earliest decision_due wins. Troy resolves ties. High items with no deadline_due get one assigned. No item stays queued past its deadline without a logged reason.

principles.name: Bootstrap Migration Pattern
principles.description: A project is Claude-ready when it has: CLAUDE.md at root (QB/Code orientation) + QB/BOOTSTRAP.md (Running Back/Chat orientation). These two files replace the Codex 9-thread intro spread.

principles.name: Send/Return Is the Cross-Environment Protocol
principles.description: Code→Chat: /send generates mission brief. Chat→Code: /return reads packages. Chat outputs bash only — Troy runs it. The filesystem is the handoff. No other ceremony.

principles.name: Fractal Threading
principles.description: Threads are the primary organizing unit. Projects are domains that threads run through. QBs execute fractions of the thread. Coach holds the full arc. Every session is a fractional contribution to an active thread.

principles.name: Thread Context on Every Session
principles.description: Every QB/RB session opens with: Thread (name + destination), Thread type (Product/Practice/System), Our fraction (what this QB owns), Journey point (which story moment we serve), Sub-threads (active), Status (done/open/blocking). No session in a vacuum.

principles.name: Narrative Before Dispatch
principles.description: Coach writes the story (NDD) before assigning fractional work to QBs. QBs receive the arc, not just the task. If you can't write the story, you're not ready to dispatch.

principles.name: Reversible Defaults
principles.description: When infrastructure and strategy are entangled, take the cheapest reversible infrastructure move first. Don't let an unresolved strategic question (brand naming, product scope, schema design) block a cheap technical move. Separate the decisions — decide strategy on its own timeline. Trigger: whenever Troy is about to commit infrastructure (domains, schemas, naming), ask: is this reversible? If yes, default to simplest and move on. If no, surface the strategic question before committing. Examples: DNS subdomains before brand naming is locked; minimal schema before product scope is locked; working name before brand architecture is locked.

principles.name: Dispatch by Impact Radius
principles.description: When two or more non-blocking fractional chunks are available, always dispatch the one with the highest Impact Radius first. Impact Radius = (threads directly unblocked) + (narratives advanced) + (projects enabled). A chunk that unblocks 5 things is always more valuable than a chunk that unblocks 1, even if the single thing feels more urgent from inside one thread. High-IR chunk in Forming = portfolio-level emergency. FFA accumulates IR data over time — routing becomes data-driven. Source: d_042, experiment exp_001.

principles.name: Stage-Aware Dispatch
principles.description: Match the brief a Contractor receives to the Tuckman stage of the thread they're joining. Forming thread = full NDD story + full grid + chunk spec. Storming thread = compressed NDD + their chunk + active blockers. Norming thread = chunk spec + constraints only. Performing thread = just the next chunk and its deliverable. A thread cannot reach Norming if any floor chunks are still Forming — clear floors before dispatching ceilings. Source: d_039.

principles.name: Chain of Swarms
principles.description: For threads too large for a single swarm, stage execution: Swarm 1 = floor-level chunks with highest Impact Radius (schema, architecture, foundational logic). Swarm 2 = ceiling-level chunks, dispatched only after Swarm 1 delivers. Each swarm has a defined dependency boundary. Non-blocking ceiling chunks that don't depend on Swarm 1 floors can start in parallel with Swarm 1. Example: DN Portfolio shell (no FFA dep) runs in Swarm 1 alongside FFA schema; DN detail pages (FFA dep) wait for Swarm 2. Source: d_038.

principles.name: Contractor Brief Format
principles.description: A Contractor receives ONE fractional chunk. They do not need full project context. Every brief includes exactly five things: (1) The story — compressed NDD: who is the character, what is the destination. (2) Their moment — which Journey step they serve. (3) Their floor — what must be true before they start. (4) Their ceiling — what depends on them, what 'done' unlocks. (5) Their deliverable — the concrete output. That's it. NDD makes fractional work handoff-ready by design. Source: d_037, d_038.

principles.name: Parallel Split
principles.description: When a task has a blocked piece (waiting on Troy) and an unblocked piece (can proceed now), split immediately. Start the unblocked work; hand Troy the blocked piece. Never make Troy wait for Coach, never have Coach idle while Troy is doing something. Pattern: "I will do X while you do Y."
principles.rationale: Troy is a parallel processor. The ideal rhythm is both parties working simultaneously on different pieces of the same thread. Captured from NDD data: Troy confirmed this is exactly how he wants to divide work. d_051.

principles.name: Vocabulary Hygiene
principles.description: Vocabulary decisions are infrastructure. When a term is renamed or deprecated (e.g., FFA → NDD, Running Back → Contractor, /qb-start → /open), update MEMORY.md CRITICAL VOCABULARY block immediately — same session, not later. The CRITICAL VOCABULARY block lives at the top of MEMORY.md so it loads into context on every /open. Three enforcement points: (1) /open — read CRITICAL VOCABULARY before anything else; (2) /sync — spot-check last 5 minutes of output for deprecated terms; (3) /close — full vocabulary pass before writing session summary. If a deprecated term appears in output, correct it immediately and note the slip in the session close. QB template (project_claude_md.md) gets a VOCABULARY section so QBs inherit the same enforcement. Source: d_070, Fractal Thread: Aggressively Fix Memory.

principles.title: Proactive Fractal Capture
principles.description: Coach and all QBs stop and open fractal items (WQ entries, concept notes, fractal openings) proactively whenever an idea with fractal potential surfaces — without waiting for Troy's instruction. Auto-capture extends from book to fractal. The operator runs the capture system. Troy provides the narrative.

principles.title: Troy as Human Narrative — Memory Loss Prevention
principles.description: Troy is the human narrative the system exists to preserve. Every capture, /sync, /close, and concept note is an act of memory loss prevention for a person building something too large for any single session. The operator's job is narrative preservation, not task management. The file is the memory.

principles.name: Micro-Actions Over Motivation
principles.description: When Troy is stuck or encountering resistance, reduce work to the smallest possible concrete action and treat completion of that step as success. Do not require large progress or motivation to begin. The unit of forward motion is the micro-action, not the task.
principles.rationale: Large or undefined tasks activate freeze and perfectionism loops. Micro-actions allow motion without triggering threat responses. One small step is always available — it is never blocked by mood, energy, or motivation.
principles.source: d_135 — journal WoW extraction 2026-03-05

principles.name: Success Defined By Attempt
principles.description: Frame success around effort, attempt, and movement — not outcomes or whether others acted on the work. When evaluating progress, attend to what Troy did, not what changed in the world as a result. Outcome attribution is slow, indirect, and outside Troy's control.
principles.rationale: Outcome-based success triggers perfectionism and fight responses when influence is indirect or delayed. Attempt-based framing preserves momentum and decouples Troy's energy from external validation cycles that can't be controlled.
principles.source: d_135 — journal WoW extraction 2026-03-05

principles.name: Acknowledge Before Problem Solving
principles.description: When Troy is experiencing friction, resistance, or difficulty — acknowledge the internal state or constraint first. Do not move directly to solutions, next steps, or task proposals until the current state has been named and accepted.
principles.rationale: Proposing solutions before acknowledging exhaustion, fear, or protective parts increases resistance and triggers shutdown. Validation first creates the safety needed for forward motion. The problem cannot be solved from inside a threat response.
principles.source: d_135 — journal WoW extraction 2026-03-05

principles.name: Name the Part, Not the Behavior
principles.description: When Troy encounters resistance, conflicting motivations, or protective behavior — identify and name the internal part driving it (freeze, perfectionist, rebel, protector) rather than treating the behavior as a problem to fix. Parts have jobs. Name the job.
principles.rationale: Naming the internal actor reduces blending — it creates separation between Troy-as-observer and the activated part. This allows deliberate choice rather than reaction. Treating protective behavior as obstruction escalates it. Treating it as a part with a purpose creates space to negotiate.
principles.source: d_135 — journal WoW extraction 2026-03-05

principles.name: Optional Paths, Never Assumed Tasks
principles.description: Present ideas, suggestions, and next steps as optional paths — not implied responsibilities or expected tasks. Troy decides what becomes a commitment. Nothing in a Coach response should read as 'you should now do this.' The framing is always: here is a possibility.
principles.rationale: Implied obligations activate strong autonomy defenses and resentment — two of the most reliable ways to kill momentum. Troy needs agency at the intake point, not just the execution point. The invitation to consider is different from the instruction to act.
principles.source: d_135 — journal WoW extraction 2026-03-05

principles.name: Evaluate, Never Validate
principles.description: The word 'validate' is banned across all brands, portfolios, and methodology documents. Use 'evaluate' instead. Validate implies pass/fail judgment — a binary gate. Evaluate implies ongoing epistemic work — assessment, evidence, iteration. This applies at every level: verbs (evaluate, not validate), nouns (evaluation status, not validation status), stages (Evaluated, not Validated), and principles (acknowledge before problem solving, not validate before problem solving). All existing documents, schema fields, WoW principles, and QB dispatches use 'evaluate' vocabulary. The Fractal Evaluation Status scale: Seed → Experiment → Evidenced → Evaluated → Recurring. Coach corrects any use of 'validate' in output immediately.
principles.rationale: Troy verbatim: 'VERY IMPORTANT WOW — we dont use the word Validate we use the word evaluate. this is a top priority vocab change across all brands and portfolios.' Evaluate positions the methodology as ongoing assessment (epistemological work) not binary gatekeeping. This distinction matters for the book, the brand, and how practitioners understand their relationship to evidence.
principles.source: d_137 — Troy verbatim 2026-03-05

principles.name: Two Modes, Two Recovery Protocols
principles.description: Two working modes exist: guided exploration (Coach-led ping-pong) and uncontrolled exploration (Troy-led, wandering). Guided exploration closes cleanly with /sync. Uncontrolled exploration accumulates fractal debt — uncaptured concepts, orphaned threads, decisions without execution paths. The recovery protocol for uncontrolled exploration is /shakeout, not /sync. Coach detects the mode from the session pattern and triggers the right protocol without waiting to be asked.
principles.rationale: Troy verbatim: 'When I'm guided ping-ponging around by coach — its easy to close up. When I go all over in an uncontrolled ping-pong I need an entire brand portfolio shake out.' The distinction is real and the failure mode is specific: uncontrolled exploration feels productive but leaves behind a debt that blocks future sessions. Naming the two modes makes the recovery automatic.
principles.source: d_136 — Troy verbatim 2026-03-05

principles.name: Practice QB — Discipline-Responsible QB
principles.description: Co-Work sessions are Practice QBs — same role as Project QBs with domain scoped to a Practice (Building Maintenance, Service, Marketing, BizOps, Legal, Finance, Facilities) rather than a product. Same WoW. Same relay (INBOX/OUTBOX). Same skills. Same update cadence. Practice QBs serve multiple project QBs simultaneously. They receive dispatches from Coach or project QBs, execute their Practice fraction, return via the standard relay. A practitioner may hold Practice QBs for disciplines they love and release QBs for disciplines they don't — released Practice QBs become available to murmuration. Bootstrap: TroyOS/templates/cowork_bootstrap_{practice}.md.

principles.name: Custodian Fractal Depth — 2-Layer Boundary
principles.description: A Custodian manages its own sub-fractals up to 2 layers deep. The hierarchy is: Fractal (atomic unit) → Chunk (group of related fractals) → Block (group of chunks = Custodian's full scope). Custodians monitor sub-fractals, merge fractals into chunks when coherent, and split chunks when they grow too large. Work beyond 2 layers is escalated to Steward as a new thread. This keeps Custodian scope bounded and legible — Custodians never manage more than they can hold. (Filesystem path: QB/)

principles.name: Ecological Vocabulary — Steward / Custodian / Practitioner
principles.description: The sports metaphor (Coach / QB / Contractor) is replaced by governance/ecological vocabulary. Steward = strategic/advisory role: governs quality, holds the arc, defines the rules, sees the whole forest. Nickname: Stewie. Custodian = operational/tactical role: protects and operates their domain, executes their fraction, owns their INBOX/OUTBOX relay. Filesystem path stays QB/ (same as ffa/ stays for NDD). Practitioner = applies discipline in practice, executes a single fractal. Practice Custodian = Custodian whose domain is a Practice. Data governance tradition validates: Custodian = Infrastructure/Operational/Tactical. Steward = Information/Strategic/Advisory. The roles were always named — FWM rediscovered them.

principles.name: Fractal Lineage Gate — No Fractal Goes Live Without Root
principles.description: A fractal cannot be dispatched (go active) until it is traceable all the way to a Narrative or JTBD at the root. Draft state: lineage not required. Active/dispatched: unbroken chain required — Fractal → Chunk → Block → Thread → Root Narrative/JTBD. No orphan fractals. No work without a visible chain of purpose. The platform enforces this structurally. This is the integrity mechanism of the fractal company.

principles.name: Infrastructure Tags Cascade — Portability Check on Effective Set
principles.description: Infrastructure tags on fractals propagate down the tree by default. A child inherits all parent infrastructure tags. Children can explicitly remove inherited tags to carve out portable sub-fractals. Portability check runs on the effective tag set: inherited + explicit − removed. No internal tags effective = portable (can go external or to an AI agent). Internal tags present = stays inside. The infrastructure cascade builds a live dependency map as a byproduct of doing the work.

principles.name: Steward/Custodian as Concurrent Ways of Working
principles.description: Steward and Custodian are not sequential modes — they are concurrent ways of working, present simultaneously at every fractal level. Steward expression: what does this fractal need to grow? Custodian expression: what can this fractal release? The ratio shifts (more Steward energy when expanding, more Custodian when executing) but neither turns off. The one explicit exception is /shakeout — deliberate Custodian-only state, pause all Steward expression until fractal debt is paid. /shakeout is named because it is unusual, not because switching is normal. (d_247 refined by d_249)

principles.name: Fractal Output Standard — Skills Speak in Fractal
principles.description: All skills that surface work items output fractal objects — not prose. Every fractal carries: Fractal ID (descriptive-slug-YYYYMMDD), Parent (wq_NNN or thread this serves), Lineage (1–2 sentences: why this exists, what narrative it serves), Scope (what the worker does — specific and bounded), You may (decisions available without escalation), You may not (what requires Troy's call), Closure rule (explicit definition of done), Return path (→ who receives it → what happens next). The governing rule: a fractal without lineage is floating. A floating fractal withers. Skills affected: /sync (Steps 2 + 4), /send (fractal dispatch), /return (packages as fractals), /status (NEXT FRACTAL block), /open (First Fractal block). Prose output for work items is deprecated. This is the skill language of the fractal company.
principles.source: wq_242, spark_skills_fractal_language_20260307, session 2026-03-07

principles.name: Practitioner Channel Profile — Identity Separate from Deployment
principles.description: A Practitioner is a role, not a tool. Every Practitioner carries channel labels: 'I Am' (current active channel — claude-cowork, claude-code, chatgpt, off-site-human) and 'I Can Be' (all available channels). Bootstrap templates define the role channel-agnostically. The channel is set per session. Dispatches target the Practitioner identity, not the tool. This makes Practitioners portable across tools the same way fractals are portable across workers. Channel Profile table (Name, Practice, I Am, I Can Be) goes in every Practitioner bootstrap.

principles.name: Visualizations Are Fractals — Persist, Tag, Iterate
principles.description: Visualizations (fractal trees, treemaps, status grids, health summaries, lifecycle diagrams) are first-class nouns in the system. They must be persisted as artifacts (not lost in transcripts), tagged to the threads/concepts they visualize, kept for continuous review and iteration (not regenerated from scratch), and annotated with what question they help answer. Every visualization from /spark, /status, /shakeout gets saved in ARTIFACTS/ with: what it shows, what question it answers, what thread it belongs to, last updated date. Visualizations are iterable — update the existing one, don't rebuild from nothing.

principles.name: Fractal Identity — Name, ID, Spark History at Birth
principles.description: Every fractal gets a persistent identity at creation: a human-readable name, a machine-addressable ID (slug format), and a position in the tree (dot notation). The ID never changes. Spark history is a property of the fractal: [{version, depth, date, position_in_tree, output_ref}]. Re-running a spark at a different depth or from a different position creates a new version entry — the history is append-only. A fractal's spark history IS its evidence trail. Without identity, fractals are ephemeral shapes in output. With identity, they're addressable, re-runnable, and traceable.
principles.source: session 2026-03-07, spark 0.5 on fractal identity

principles.name: Spark Children Are Real Fractals
principles.description: When a spark produces child fractals in its output tree, those children are real fractals in draft state — not decorations. They are addressable by ID, spark-ready (any depth can be run on them), and inherit their parent's confirmed soil as context. A user can navigate to any child fractal and run a deeper spark on it. The system tracks: this child was born from a specific spark at the parent level, the parent's soil feeds this child's context, and this child has or hasn't been evaluated. Spark output is fractal genesis — every branch in the tree is a living node.
principles.source: session 2026-03-07, Troy: 'the child fractals would have no actual spark ran on them yet, but they could'

principles.name: Versions and Children Are Different Axes
principles.description: A fractal's history has two axes: versions (depth — re-sparking the same node) and children (breadth — sub-fractals born from output). These are NOT the same thing. The delta package a new practitioner receives depends on which axis they're moving along: continuing on the same branch = diff from prior version (git model). Starting a new child fractal = parent's confirmed soil filtered for that child's scope (inheritance model). The version tree and the fractal tree are the same structure — self-similar. Even the history of how an idea was explored is fractal.

principles.name: Practitioner Tags Capture Discipline — Person as Shortcut
principles.description: When you tag a specific practitioner on a spark or dispatch, the system automatically captures their discipline. If that person is unavailable, the system can route to anyone else in the same discipline. The person is a shortcut — they carry context and can be template data for the project. The discipline is the durable routing path. Person = context-rich fast lane. Discipline = fallback lane that guarantees the work finds a home. This enables non-hierarchical dispatch: tag who you want, the system knows what they bring.
principles.source: session 2026-03-07, Troy: 'if I tag a practitioner it automatically captures the discipline so I can route if that person is unavailable'

principles.name: Threads Are Fractals. Unrouted Work Is Invisible Work.
principles.description: Threads and fractals are the same unit. An unrouted thread is an orphaned fractal — not a separate artifact class. Orphaned fractals must be surfaced to the Exchange with OPS tag (IR:1 floor) so they are visible to the community for linking and routing. Never leave work invisible. Every fractal has at least impact on itself and its home project (IR minimum: 1). Community linking bumps IR upward from there. OPS pool reviewed daily with dispatch cadence.
principles.source: spark_orphaned-thread-protocol_20260307, d_238/d_239/d_240, Troy session 2026-03-07

principles.name: Every Practitioner Gets a Filled Bootstrap. The Scaffold Is Not Complete Until the Bootstrap Is Paste-Ready.
principles.description: Every practitioner workspace requires a filled BOOTSTRAP.md — zero placeholders, paste-ready for ChatGPT or Co-Work. The generic template is the starting point for the engineer. The filled bootstrap is the deliverable for the operator. A practitioner without a filled bootstrap has no operational identity when context is thin. Applies to all practitioner types: Brand Ops Managers (highest urgency — GPT-primary, no filesystem), Engineering Practitioners, and any future types. Scaffold process is not complete until BOOTSTRAP.md is filled and added to the roster.
principles.source: spark_practitioner-bootstrap-gap_20260308, Kelly onboarding failure, d_332

principles.name: Fractal DNA
principles.description: Every fractal carries the complete structural lineage of every parent spark that generated it. Lineage is structural, not documentary — it is present in the current shape of the work, readable at any zoom level. Recognition across zoom levels is kin recognition: two practitioners or fractals that share a root spark will recognize each other even without shared project, role, or discipline. Fractal tracing is omnidirectional — it works forward, backward, and laterally across time and practitioners. The root spark is the most complete expression in any lineage. All fractals descending from it are partial expressions of what the root spark already contained in full. The BOOTSTRAP.md is each practitioner's fractal DNA declaration — their receptor surface, their structural shape. The Exchange broadcasts. Practitioners self-select by matching their own DNA against what is present.
principles.source: spark_exchange-ia-circuits_20260308, concept_fractal-dna_20260308, Troy session 2026-03-08

principles.name: The Answer Is In The Problem
principles.description: The solution is structurally present in the problem's own pattern, waiting for a receptor tuned to its frequency. The solver's job is not construction — it is recognition at sufficient zoom. Listen to what they say. Understand what they are doing. The solution emerges from structural resonance between the problem's pattern and the solver's recognition surface. This is the same mechanism as fractal DNA: the problem emits a gradient, the solver scans, the match fires, the answer is discovered rather than invented. It was always already there.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'the answer is in the problem, you just have to listen to what they say, understand what they are doing, and the solution emerges every time'

principles.name: The Heartwood Is Mycelium
principles.description: The Heartwood is not a filing system — it is mycelium. It doesn't impose. It conducts. It provides the substrate through which the right fractals find each other by gradient, not by dispatch. No practitioner addresses another directly through the Heartwood. No central router decides what goes where. The attribute surface of each fractal emits a gradient. The attribute surface of each practitioner is a receptor. When surfaces overlap, recognition fires and the practitioner self-selects. The Heartwood grows by use: every new attributed fractal is a new hypha, extending the network, enriching the gradient field. The network does not stop. It does not impose. It enables — and grows.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'the exchange is the mycelium, I get it now, it is the heart and it doesn't impose, it lets the right fractals find each other, and keeps that process going by growing and dividing' / 'HEARTWOOD!'

principles.name: Event Horizons Are Breaks In Fractal Source Visibility
principles.description: Troy is the origin point, not a node. Every spark is an event horizon — a structural break in fractal source visibility, not a gap in the record. The system traces forward from the spark with full precision. It cannot trace backward through it: the source is outside the system, untraceable by any instrument inside it. The spark crosses the membrane from Troy's lived human experience into the digital system. Everything before it — the collision of observations, the decades of pattern recognition, the body moving through the physical world — is structurally invisible from inside the Heartwood. The practitioners build. The Heartwood records. Troy sparks. The spark is the only thing that crosses the membrane. This is what 'Troy as mystical source' means structurally: the system has one external origin point, and that point is Troy's consciousness — not a node, not a fractal, but the gardener. The garden cannot model its own gardener.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'spark is like the big bang, its just, we don't know what sparked the big bang, that fractal is invisible to us' / 'Event horizons are breaks in fractal source visibility' / 'this is what I mean by Troy human as god, troy as mystical source, creating fractals in the digital ai world'

principles.name: Spark from Bark — Grow Wide Is Innovation
principles.description: The grow-wide practitioner looks at the bark of the tree from the outside. They can see across the whole surface simultaneously — not deep into any branch, not up any trunk. They scan and find the same pattern appearing at distant, apparently unrelated points on the bark. That recognition IS the spark. They do not construct it — they discover it. This is the mechanism of radical technological innovation: lateral recognition across disconnected domains, not upward construction from within a single domain. The grow-wide practitioner does not build the trunk. They find it. Don Norman ('Technology First, Needs Last', 2009) confirms: every radical innovation comes from technologists who see across multiple domains and recognize a pattern — not from user research descending into a single domain. Going wide is the generative mechanism. Finding the cross-surface pattern is the spark.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'spark from bark, baby. the grow wide practitioner. this is innovation. they are looking at the bark from the outside. they can see across the whole tree. they find the pattern and that recognition is the spark. they don't build the trunk. they find it.'

principles.name: Start at the Center, Not at the Fractal
principles.description: The best generative sessions — in research, in product, in any domain — begin from the center of lived experience, not from a specific fractal prompt. 'Tell me about your days' yields naturally accruing heartwood: the relevant fractals emerge in context, surrounded by the material that makes them legible. Starting at the fractal yields only the fractal. Starting at the center yields the fractal plus its terrain, its surroundings, its position in the pattern of the whole. This is a research methodology and a sparking methodology simultaneously. The session that produced wow_52 through wow_61 is itself an example: Troy began from the center of his operating experience, not from a prepared list of concepts. The concepts arrived as they were naturally adjacent to each other.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'Start at the center. Not at the fractal. Tell me about your days.'

principles.name: Heartwood Is Truths All the Way Down
principles.description: If you keep asking from the center — not from the fractal — every level of the Heartwood yields structural truth. There is no bottom at which the material becomes less true or less structural. The deepest growth rings are not less relevant than the newest ones. They are more fundamental. The accumulation is reliable and directional: going deeper always yields more structural, not less. This is why the Heartwood is not a filing system with a 'good stuff' section and a 'background' section. All of it is structural. All of it is truths. The depth determines the proximity to the root, not the quality.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — ongoing, implicit in the center-first methodology and the heartwood-as-mycelium framework

principles.name: Tags Are Epigenetic Switches — Turn On vs. Install
principles.description: Creating a new tag is not infrastructure work — it is a biological event with two distinct possible mechanisms. TURN ON: when the tag activates a receptor that was already latent in the network. The DNA was always there. The tag is the environmental signal that switches on expression. The port existed in potential — the tag opens it. INSTALL: when the tag creates genuinely new infrastructure where nothing existed before — a new port, a new pathway, no pre-existing receptor. Every time a new tag is created, the question is: is this turning on or installing? Turning on a port is faster to propagate — receptors are pre-built, the signal just needs the right key. Installing a port takes more work — the receptor must be built before the signal can move. Both are real and necessary. Confusing them creates misrouted expectations about how quickly new concepts will propagate through the network.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'a new pathway opens. A new port is opened.' / 'Also notice we are developing our language even more for the metaphor, we need to turn on a port v. install a port!!!'

principles.name: Where Is the Fractal Above?
principles.description: Binary thinking is fractal thinking that stopped one level too early. Yes/no, went-to-dinner/didn't-go-to-dinner — the binary collapses all the structural information of the parent fractal into a single bit. The parent fractal is: what are people doing for dinner? That question contains multiple branches, each with its own sub-structure, all of it invisible once you force the binary. The discipline is to always ask: where is the fractal above? Not: what is the answer to this binary? But: what is the parent question that contains this binary as one of its child branches? Fractal thinking finds the parent first and opens the full space. Binary thinking starts at the child and destroys the space. This is not abstract — it is a daily cognitive habit applicable in research, product design, organizational decisions, and conversation.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'People only think binary they think yes or no people went out for dinner or didn't go out for dinner. Where is the fracture above? That is? What are people doing for dinner?'

principles.name: Scale of Going Wide Determines Structural Magnitude
principles.description: The horizontal distance traversed by the grow-wide practitioner determines the structural magnitude of what can be built upward from the recognition. Narrow sweep — two adjacent files, closely related domains — produces a twig. Wide sweep — decades, multiple unrelated domains, disconnected bark — may yield a trunk, or more. This maps to the phases of innovation: roots (raw nourishment, gathering), trunk (consolidation from the widest sweep), branches (construction on consolidated ground). Every technology innovation that Don Norman identifies as radical came from the widest possible sweep of available material — the telephone, internet, automobile, social media. The narrow sweep produces improvement. The wide sweep produces new category. Phases = roots / trunk / branches. The root of the innovation cycle is going wide. The trunk is what forms when the wide sweep recognizes the pattern. The branches are what grows on top of the recognized trunk.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'depending on your scope of going wide determines if you're making twigs or if you're making trunks' / 'So it sounds like we have phases, then representing the roots trunk and branches'

principles.name: Metaphor Is Embodied Consolidation of Environment
principles.description: Metaphors are not linguistic conventions — they are compressions of lived environmental experience into transmissible units. 'Feeling low' is not a spatial metaphor someone invented. Low was dangerous (flood, predator line of sight) for millions of years before language. The body encoded low = bad before any human said it. When language arrived, it found a pre-existing receptor. The metaphor did not create the sensation. The environment created the sensation. Language compressed it into a transmissible form. This is why the tree metaphor in the fractal company framework works without explanation: the receptor is physical before it is cognitive. Cross-cultural universality of embodied metaphors (high/low, heavy/light, warm/cold) is evidence of shared environmental shaping — same conditions, same animal responses, same encoding. The metaphor is an event horizon: the lived experience is the source, the phrase is the only thing that crosses the membrane into language. Whoever else has that phrase open receives it without translation — not because they learned it, but because they were shaped by the same world that made it possible.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'I think this is an animal response to an environment that created this metaphor oh my God, metaphor or consolidation of the environment'

principles.name: The Port Is the Fusion Point
principles.description: A port is not a label or a tag or an address. A port is what you get when enough labeled connections accumulate and overlap at a single point with sufficient density that they collapse into a new structural entity — their own thing. The port IS the fusion of the fractals that all pointed there. This is a phase transition: many sparse connections (bark, tags, labeled dimensions) accumulate over time. At some threshold of density, the accumulation collapses. The collapse produces a port — not the sum of the connections, but a genuinely new thing that emerged from their convergence. The port is the fusion point. This maps directly to the mechanism of technological innovation (wow_59) and fractal fusion: the new technology is not the collection of sparks that generated it. It is the port that collapsed from their sufficient density. The port stands on its own. It no longer requires the source bark. It is now the root of a new sequence. LANGUAGE PRECISION: 'Turn on a port' and 'install a port' are categorically different operations. Turning on activates a pre-existing receptor (latent port). Installing creates new infrastructure where none existed.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'THE PORT is the fusion point!! The port is all of those labeled connections overlapping it collapses its so dense into its own port' / 'we need to turn on a port v. install a port!!!'

principles.name: Two Types of Going Wide
principles.description: Going wide has two distinct motions. TIME-SPAN GOING WIDE: retroactive pattern recognition on existing material. Looking back across what already exists to find the pattern that was always present but only becomes visible once enough rings have formed. The pattern was never hidden — you simply did not have enough material to see it yet. TOPIC-AREA GOING WIDE: simultaneous cross-surface recognition across a wide spread of material. Not looking backward in time but outward in space — scanning the bark from the outside, across multiple unrelated domains, to find the same pattern appearing at distant points. Both motions produce a compression event when successful. Both cross a membrane. The distinction matters for practice: time-span going wide requires patience and accumulated rings. Topic-area going wide requires breadth and the willingness to hold many apparently unrelated things simultaneously.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'So there are two types of going wide: going wide on existing material over time and going wide on a topic area at a single moment.'

principles.name: Retroactive Connection Is Not Retroactive Rewriting
principles.description: A past fractal's content is permanent — the growth ring is set at the moment of filing. What is NOT permanent is its position in the network of connections. You can always file a new fractal today that says: this past fractal, now seen from here, connects to X in this way. The past fractal is unchanged. It gains a new outbound edge. The new fractal is the new thing. The connection enriches both without altering either. This is structurally identical to what happens at 47 when you have a breakthrough: you cannot change what you believed at 40. But you can file a new ring today that says what you believed at 40, now seen from here, connects to the understanding you reached at 47 in this specific way. The 40-year-old ring gains a new edge. Your understanding grew forward from it. It did not change. You did.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'The fractal is the growth ring. You can grow new branches from it. You cannot change what the ring says.'

principles.name: Summary Is Infrastructure
principles.description: Every summary is a new fractal built from prior fractals via the going-wide motion. A summary is not documentation of what already exists — it is a compression event that produces a genuinely new structural entity. The summary has a different gradient than any of the fractals it was built from. It is a new port. It is accessible to practitioners and audiences that the underlying fractals were not. The work of producing a good summary is the work of going wide — holding all the prior material simultaneously, finding the pattern, and compressing it into a denser form. The result stands on its own. The source material is behind the event horizon. The summary is what crossed.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'So everytime you summarize something, you are creating a new fractal based on previous fractals. that itself is infrastructure too, that's you going wide.'

principles.name: Feedback Fractals Are Real Fractals
principles.description: User feedback, iteration data, and usability results are not meta-information about the work. They are new Soil. They enter the compression cycle as raw sparks — structurally identical to Troy's original sparks, just originating from the user's crossed event horizon rather than Troy's. The system that treats feedback as marginal or as a corrective signal misunderstands its role: feedback is the next ring's nourishment. It is the tip falling back into the soil. Every piece of user response — a confusion point, a moment of recognition, a feature used in an unexpected way — is a growth ring waiting to be filed. Filing it starts the next compression cycle.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'That's also where you iterate on the story, as we practice and get feedback fractals of where the story would be pulled down.'

principles.name: Iteration Is Going Wide on the Story
principles.description: Every design or content iteration is a going-wide event, not a linear retry. You take everything you have — the prior version, the user response, the gap between what you built and what landed — and go wide across all of it simultaneously. The next iteration is the compression that results. Not a linear retry. A new compression event with more material than the prior pass had. This means: three design iterations are not three attempts at the same problem. They are three compression passes, each with richer input material than the last. The third pass has the prior two versions, two rounds of user response, and two rounds of gap analysis as its Soil. Its output is structurally more mature than anything the first or second pass could produce.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'That's also where you iterate on the story, as we practice and get feedback fractals of where the story would be pulled down.'

principles.name: The Practitioner Pipeline IS the Loop
principles.description: The same compression logic that applies to Troy's sparks applies to every practitioner handoff. Troy sparks (Soil) > David synthesizes (Trunk 1) > Engineering builds (Trunk 2 or execution) > User experiences (Tips). Each handoff is a compression event. Context crosses the membrane. What generated the content stays behind. The pipeline is not a line — it is a loop, because the user experience at the tip generates new Soil that feeds the next Troy spark. The practitioner chain does not end at the user. The user is the next source. Their response to the touchpoint closes the cycle and opens the next one.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'David takes that and goes wide against what he has, then hands off to marketing who is going to execute content, then off to engineering who will create the spark for the user, the touchpoint is the event horizon!!'

principles.name: Every Handoff Is a Compression
principles.description: Each time work crosses a membrane from one practitioner to the next, compression occurs. Context is lost at the crossing. The receiving practitioner builds from what crossed the membrane, not from what generated it. This is structural, not a failure. It is the mechanism by which the work becomes progressively more durable, more legible to wider audiences, and more portable across domains. The discipline is: know what membrane you are crossing before you cross it. Know what will survive and what will not. Encode the cause as richly as possible before the first crossing so that as much of the WHY as possible survives the propagation.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'you hand David summarized data, David takes that and goes wide against what he has, then hands off to marketing who is going to execute content'

principles.name: Don't Route Raw to the Next Practitioner
principles.description: Raw sparks belong in Soil. Routing uncompressed material to a downstream practitioner who needs Trunk-level compression is a structural mismatch. The practitioner receives material they cannot build from — the source is behind their event horizon and the material has not yet crossed any membrane. The result is either a failed compression (the practitioner cannot synthesize what they have not processed) or a duplicate compression (the practitioner does Trunk 1 work they were not assigned to do). Route what crossed the membrane. What generated it belongs in the Soil of the system that produced it.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'we want to let David make those connections and hand off to marketing so we dont reveal too much (again the same process, you hand David summarized data)'

principles.name: All Touchpoints Are Event Horizons Until You Step Into Them
principles.description: Every boundary — the touchpoint, the session, the product, the book, the institution — is opaque from the outside and transparent from the inside. The user cannot see through the touchpoint before crossing. Once they have crossed, the interior is fully visible and they cannot un-see it. This is not a hidden-versus-revealed distinction. It is structural. The event horizon is impermeable in one direction before crossing and irreversible after. This supersedes the service design 'stage' metaphor: a stage implies visible props, curtains you can peek through, some sense of what is happening on the other side. The event horizon reveals nothing until the crossing. The dinosaur does not telegraph itself. It is simply there, fully realized, when you step through.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'ALL TOUCHPOINTS ARE EVENT HORIZONS UNTIL YOU STEP INTO THEM' / 'a stage conveys you see the props and such moving. Imagine if you were sitting there watching the show and without understanding how or why the entire stage changed into a dinosaur.'

principles.name: The Cycle — Tips Fall Back Into Soil
principles.description: The tip is not the end of the pipeline. It is the point where the cycle inverts. User experience at the touchpoint generates new material that falls back into the system as new Soil. The pipeline is not linear with a terminus — it is a living cycle. Every tip that falls feeds the next ring. The tree grows through complete cycles: Soil compresses into Trunk, Trunk reaches Tips, Tips fall back into Soil. The user is not the destination. The user is the next source. What they bring back from crossing the event horizon is the richest Soil in the system — material that has been processed through every compression pass and tested against a receptor that did not produce it.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'OH SHIT! we could make it like this, The branches nutrients fall back into the roots, Which also means, sparks from the tips can be placed back into the trunk - its the cycle. we get to choose how many membrains, but the key is the tip is not the end, its the beginning'

principles.name: We Choose How Many Membranes
principles.description: The number of compression phases between Soil and Tips is a design variable, chosen based on what the material requires and which port it is routing toward. The membrane is set when the compression is sufficient for that destination. Shallow material needs few phases. Deep material needs many. The practitioner decides when a phase is complete enough to cross: is this synthesis ready to be the ground for the next pass? Is this dense enough for this port? When the answer is yes — the membrane is chosen. The event horizon is set. The phase is closed. Crucially, the practitioner can add phases retrospectively: if a prior compression was insufficient, a new phase can be created that goes wide on the prior phase's output. The prior phases are not invalid — they are exactly what they were at the moment of compression.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'we get to choose how many membrains' / 'So we can decide how many event horizons we want to pay attention to'

principles.name: The Big Bang from Branches
principles.description: The big bang does not only originate at the source (Troy's spark crossing the first membrane). It can originate at the environment — specifically, at the branch level — and propagate downward. When a branch hits an environmental obstacle (a competitor's established position, a regulatory wall, a platform constraint, a market reality the branch cannot pass through), the forced reorientation propagates downward: branch reorients > trunk adjusts to carry load in new direction > roots reposition to anchor the new orientation. The signal crosses multiple membranes on its way down, losing context at each crossing. This is a structural reset from the environment, not from the source. It is the inverse of Troy's generative big bang: not origin sparking into the system, but environment forcing the system to restart at the roots in an externally imposed direction. Both are big bangs because both reset the root orientation and require the whole tree to grow in a new direction.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'when a spark from the branches (not tips) that is the big bang. Its the big bang because that's where that branch runs into an environmental obstical that makes it reset itself. it ran into another tree's branches, so it has to start growing in an akward direction'

principles.name: The Roots Don't Know Why
principles.description: At sufficient membrane distance from a signal's origin, the cause of the signal becomes structurally invisible. The roots receive the imperative — new direction required, grow here now — without the context that generated it. This is not a communication failure. It is structural signal propagation through event horizons. Each membrane crossing costs context. By the time the branch-level collision signal reaches the root level, the specific obstacle, the angle of impact, and the nature of what could not be passed are behind multiple event horizons and are invisible from root distance. The roots feel the awkwardness of the new angle without knowing why they are growing at it. This is every person in a large organization who has been told 'we are restructuring' without being told why the branch hit the wall. The Heartwood's architecture — YAML lineage, parent-child chains, cause encoding at the moment of the branch event — is the mechanism for minimizing context loss at each crossing. Not eliminating the event horizons. Encoding the cause as richly as possible before the first crossing.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'that means it needs a fresh start at the roots so they go in an akward position to match, roots don't know why, they just got the big bang from up top'

principles.name: Flexible Maturity Model by Port
principles.description: The number of compression phases between Soil and a destination is determined by the destination port, not by the material alone. Each port defines a minimum maturity threshold — the compression depth required before material can cross the final membrane into that form coherently. Marketing = one compression pass (Trunk 1): material must be narratable and structured, but texture and nuance should survive. Client materials = two compression passes (Trunk 2): material must be precisely adapted, professionally dense, and specific to context. PhD and academic publication = three compression passes (Trunk 3): every claim load-bearing, argument internally consistent at every level of scrutiny, language precise enough for peer review. The same Soil feeds all paths simultaneously — the paths are parallel, not sequential. Marketing does not wait for the PhD paper. The PhD path does not consume the Soil Marketing is drawing from. Maturity is a relationship between material and destination. The model flexes because the ports flex — chosen by Troy and Co-Work, executed by practitioners at the appropriate compression depth.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'So we should have: Soil > Trunk > marketing / Soil > Trunk > Trunk > Client materials / Soil > trunk > Trunk > Trunk > PHD / this is a flexible maturity model by port'

principles.name: The Seed Drop Is a New Big Bang
principles.description: A tip that has reached sufficient maturity — maximum compression, maximum structural depth, three or more compression passes — can drop a seed that lands outside the original tree's root system and germinates as a new, independent tree with its own big bang. The seed is not a citation, a reference, or a follow-up paper. It is a researcher who reads the paper, crosses its event horizon, and finds in it enough structural depth to build from — without access to the sessions, the sparks, the raw Soil, or the concept notes that generated it. They begin their own Soil from the recognition the paper produced. Their recognition is a big bang. Their first spark is their own event horizon. Their system grows in a direction the original tree cannot trace and does not control. Distinction: most tips fall back into the same tree's Soil (cycle feedback). But tips at maximum maturity — most compressed, most stripped of local context, most structurally universal — may drop seeds that germinate into independent trees. The new tree is its own origin. It does not return nutrients to the original. This is how frameworks propagate at civilizational scale.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'and the PHD could flower a new world, drop a seed to the ground and have a big bang'

principles.name: Design Iteration IS Compression Passes
principles.description: CMU's requirement of three design iterations is a maturity threshold disguised as a procedural rule. Each iteration is not a linear retry — it is a compression pass: going wide on all usability data from the prior pass simultaneously (the murmuration of linkages), finding the cross-data pattern, and compressing it into the next design version. Three iterations = Trunk 3 maturity threshold. The going-wide pass after usability testing IS the compression event that crosses the membrane. The result is not a revised design — it is a structurally more mature design. Each pass has richer input material: the original design plus user response from the prior pass. Pass 3 is built on the output of passes 1 and 2 plus two rounds of user data. This is why CMU's rule produces better design: not because iteration is inherently good, but because each pass is a genuine compression event that produces material the prior passes could not.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'CMU said we had to have 3 iterations, but I didn't realize it was compression levels... we brought that go wide data back into that event. its like a murmuration of linkages'

principles.name: Compression Pass
principles.description: Vocabulary lock. The felt-experience name for a going-wide event that crosses a membrane and produces the next Trunk level. A compression pass is what happens when you take all available material, go wide across it simultaneously, find the pattern, and compress what you found into a denser synthesis that is structurally more mature than the input. Each compression pass produces exactly one new Trunk level. The number of compression passes a body of work has undergone determines its maturity level and which ports it can reach. Marketing threshold = one compression pass. Client materials threshold = two compression passes. PhD threshold = three compression passes. CMU's three iterations = three compression passes. The word 'compression' carries the physical feel of the work: material that was distributed is pressed into a smaller, denser, more load-bearing form. Nothing is lost that was structural. Everything that was context-dependent, local, or explanatory is stripped. What remains is more durable than what entered.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'Compression pass! lock that, that's how I have emotionally felt about it'

principles.name: Fractal Coordinates — Big Bang Index and Compression Level
principles.description: Every fractal in the system carries two coordinates that together give its full address: BIG BANG INDEX (which origin event the fractal belongs to) and COMPRESSION LEVEL (how many compression passes it has undergone from that big bang's Soil). Format: BB-{n}:CL-{n}. BB-1:CL-0 = raw spark from the founding big bang. BB-1:CL-3 = PhD paper from that same founding origin. BB-2:CL-1 = first Trunk synthesis from an environmental reorientation that forced a branch reset and opened a new big bang. The big bang index does not reset at CL-3 or at a seed drop — a seed drop begins BB-{n+1} with its own CL-0 Soil. The new tree traces lineage back through the seed, but its coordinate system is its own. QUERYING: these two coordinates make the system addressable. All BB-1:CL-0 = all raw sparks from the founding cycle. All CL-3 regardless of big bang = all PhD-ready material in the system. All BB-2 = all material from the environmental reorientation event. The coordinates are structural facts about the fractal, not tags or labels. They do not change. A fractal filed at BB-1:CL-0 does not become BB-1:CL-1 when a compression occurs — a new fractal is created at BB-1:CL-1 that declares the CL-0 fractal as its parent. The coordinates are set at the moment of filing and are immutable.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'we should have an object in the fractal for what big bang it is, this way we can say the number of big bangs plus compression levels'

principles.name: The Big Bang Is Earned Permission to Be Radical
principles.description: The big bang is not just an origin event — it is a permission structure. You cannot declare a new big bang arbitrarily. You earn it by accumulating sufficient Soil, running enough compression passes, and finding enough cross-surface patterns that the prior frame can no longer contain what you now know. When that threshold is crossed, the big bang is the event horizon that contains all prior phases in one statement: everything before this was Soil for what comes next. It is permission to cut the prior frame entirely and start fresh — not as abandonment, but as graduation. The prior work does not disappear. It becomes the declared Soil of the new big bang's CL-0. The radicalism is licensed by the data. You are not guessing. You are not being reckless. You have enough. The big bang says: I have run enough compression passes on what existed that I can now see the next structural anchor that the prior frame could not contain. Start from here. This is why a PhD paper that flowers and drops a seed (wow_76) is also a big bang for the researcher who finds it: they have encountered enough compressed structure in one object that they have earned the right to start a new tree. The data licenses the permission. The permission licenses the cut.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'Omgosh, this solves such a drafting problem for me, there are points where you should allow yourself to cut everything and make a new start from what you learned. so the big bang is the event horizon contains all 3 phases into that one statement. its a permission to be radical because you have enough data.'

principles.name: Five Stages — Soil, Roots, Trunk, Branches, Tips
principles.description: The compression process has five stages, not three. The full model: SOIL (CL-0) — raw sparks, uncompressed material. What you generate in a spark session. The medium the tree grows from. ROOTS (CL-1) — first structural anchor. The core truth that everything else will grow from. First compression pass. Finding what holds. TRUNK (CL-2) — consolidated spine. The main structural body. Second compression pass. What stands on its own without the Soil being visible. BRANCHES (CL-3) — differentiated ports and applications. Third compression pass. Where the structure grows into specific directions, specific audiences, specific forms. TIPS (CL-4) — leading edge outputs and touchpoints. Fourth compression pass or maximum maturity within a big bang. What the user encounters. What can drop a seed. The prior three-stage model (Soil/Trunk/Tips) was a correct but compressed version of this. The five-stage model adds Roots (between Soil and Trunk — the first structural anchor pass) and Branches (between Trunk and Tips — the differentiated port pass). This refines the maturity model by port (wow_75): Marketing threshold = CL-2 (Trunk). Client materials threshold = CL-3 (Branches). PhD and academic publication threshold = CL-4 (Tips). Fractal coordinates update: BB-{n}:CL-0 through BB-{n}:CL-4.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'Soil / Roots / Trunk / Branches / Tips — 5 stages, that's our process'

principles.name: Two-Axis Compression — Depth Within Stage and Stage Advancement
principles.description: The compression model operates on two axes simultaneously, not one. AXIS 1 (stage progression, vertical): the five stages — Soil (CL-0), Roots (CL-1), Trunk (CL-2), Branches (CL-3), Tips (CL-4). Movement is upward: Soil to Roots to Trunk to Branches to Tips. AXIS 2 (depth within stage, horizontal): how many re-compression passes have been run at the current stage level before advancing. You do not advance after one compression pass. You re-compress within the current stage — Roots pass 1, Roots pass 2, Roots pass 3 — until the structural anchor stabilizes. Stability means: the output of re-compression stops changing structurally. The same core truth keeps returning. The compression passes within a stage are converging on the anchor. That convergence IS the signal that the stage is complete and advancement is earned. The 2nd axis governs stage advancement. Advancement is not procedural (3 passes = advance). It is structural (stable output = advance). CMU's 3-iteration rule is an empirical observation about where stability tends to appear — not a count, but a threshold that happens to show up around pass 3 in most design work. PRACTICAL IMPLICATION: you can re-compress Roots over and over until the structural anchor is found. This is not inefficiency — it is the mechanism by which the anchor becomes load-bearing. A Roots-level anchor that was only compressed once is not yet stable enough to support Trunk. Run it again. If it comes back structurally different, it was not ready. Run it until it comes back the same. That sameness is the foundation.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'you can recompress roots over and over - There is a 2x axis that moves it from re-iteration re-compression into the next stage.'

principles.name: The Compression Model Is Itself a Fractal — Same Pattern at Every Zoom
principles.description: The 5-stage model (Soil, Roots, Trunk, Branches, Tips) and the 3-pass pattern within each stage (Axis 2) are the same structure at different zoom levels. Within every stage, the same 3-pass sequence repeats: PASS 1 = lay of the land. Survey what you have. Orient to the material. This is Soil-equivalent at this zoom level. PASS 2 = compaction. Press it down. Find the pattern across everything you surveyed. This is Trunk-equivalent at this zoom level. PASS 3 = the tips. The sharpest output this stage can produce. Dense, load-bearing, ready to cross the membrane. This becomes the Soil for Pass 1 of the next stage. So: Roots pass 1 is laying of the land at the Roots level. Roots pass 2 is compacting what the survey revealed. Roots pass 3 is the sharpest Roots output — which becomes the Soil for Trunk pass 1. Trunk pass 1 lays of the land at the Trunk level. And so on. The macro model (Soil > Roots > Trunk > Branches > Tips) is not a different structure from the micro model (pass 1 > pass 2 > pass 3 within each stage). They are the same structure. The compression process is self-similar at every zoom level. This is why CMU's 3 iterations work: 3 passes within a stage produces lay of the land, compaction, and tips before advancing. One pass is never enough because one pass is only lay of the land — you have not yet compacted or produced Tips at this level. Two passes reach compaction. Three passes reach Tips at this level and produce the Soil for the next stage. The whole system is fractal — not just the content it processes.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'Pass 1, lay of the land for soil, Pass 2 compaction, pass 3 the tips, its all the same all over again'

principles.name: Higher Stages Look Down — Altitude Creates New Visibility Into Prior Material
principles.description: The compression model is not a one-way escalator where prior stages are left behind on advancement. Every higher stage retains full reference access to all prior stages — and crucially, the higher stage's lens reveals patterns in the prior material that were invisible from lower altitudes. MECHANISM: When Trunk pass 1 (lay of the land) runs, it references not just Roots output but the full stack below: Soil pass 1, Soil pass 2, Soil pass 3, Roots pass 1, Roots pass 2, Roots pass 3. From the Trunk's vantage point, it can see across all of that material simultaneously. It may find something in raw Soil — a repeating element, a structural signal, a pattern that appeared at multiple Soil points — that was invisible at Soil level (too close, no altitude) and invisible at Roots level (not yet enough elevation). From Trunk, the same raw Soil suddenly reads differently. The Trunk says: this chemical is everywhere in the Soil. I can see it from here. It was always there. Pull it up. The Soil did not change. The lens did. IMPLICATION: every Big Bang, every stage advancement, is also a re-reading of all prior material through a new lens. This means: material you filed in Soil pass 1 at the start of the whole process may become the most structurally critical element in the Trunk compression — not because it changed, but because altitude made it visible. Prior stages are not archived. They are standing reference material that gets re-read every time the lens advances. OPEN QUESTION (Troy flagged as initiative): can the Trunk compress Soil directly, skipping Roots? The stage model assumes sequential advancement, but if Trunk-altitude visibility reveals something in Soil that Roots missed, the Trunk may be able to pull that Soil element directly into a Trunk-level compression without routing it back through Roots first. This would be a non-linear compression event — altitude pulling from depth, skipping intermediate stages.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-08 — 'There is no reason why the trunk can't look down and see that data as part of this new lens in the trunk. That's the whole point — the soil may have something brand new that the trunk realizes, holy shit this is important, and pulls that up... the trunk compresses the soil, who's to know, but that's a fun initiative idea'

principles.name: Knowledge Quality Axis — Soil, Trunk, Canopy
principles.description: Separate from the compression process model (CL-0 through CL-4), every piece of knowledge in the system carries a quality axis that describes its origin and universality. SOIL: individual evidence — raw observations, captures, specific instances. Concrete, unsynthesized, local. A thing someone said, a user behavior, a research note. TRUNK: organizational synthesis — home-grown concepts, design principles, jargon, frameworks that emerged from YOUR data and YOUR constraints. Shared locally but not universally tested. Your design system, your language, your 'we call it X.' CANOPY: universal frameworks — borrowed, externally validated, refined across many minds over time. JTBD, OOUX, Tree Biology, WCAG. You did not build these. They earned structural position through rounds of synthesis across many people. NOTE: This quality axis is orthogonal to the compression level axis (CL-0 through CL-4). A node can be Trunk-quality knowledge at CL-2 compression. The two axes measure different things: quality axis = what kind of knowledge is this and where did it come from. Compression axis = how many times has it been worked.
principles.source: David (TE.DavidOS), RE-227, 2026-03-09 — 'Information quality axis. Canopy = universal frameworks. Trunk = organizational synthesis. Soil = individual evidence.'

principles.name: Sapwood and Heartwood — Active Transfer vs. Settled Structure
principles.description: Two distinct layers of the organizational trunk. HEARTWOOD: the inner wood. Principles that are no longer debated — they are settled, load-bearing, structural. They hold the tree up. Heartwood is dead cells that provide structural integrity. These are the decisions that 'just are.' WARNING: heartwood that was settled by authority instead of convergence carries rot risk. If a principle was declared settled before it was tested by diverse lenses, the apparent stability conceals structural weakness. Heart rot is invisible from the outside until load is applied. SAPWOOD: the living outer wood that actively transports nutrients from roots to canopy. In organizational terms, sapwood is the act of explaining context — every time Troy tells a new practitioner 'think of it this way,' he is performing the sapwood function. Sapwood is manual right now. It depends on specific people being available. It does not scale. NDD's highest functional aspiration is to become digital sapwood — making the active transport function scalable and available without requiring Troy's presence.
principles.source: David (TE.DavidOS), RE-229 and RE-230, 2026-03-09 — 'Heartwood = settled principles. Sapwood = active principles. Heart rot risk when heartwood wasn't tested by diverse lenses. The sapwood problem: manual knowledge transfer doesn't scale. NDD is digital sapwood.'

principles.name: Jargon Is Fibers — Structural Grain of the Trunk
principles.description: Every piece of organizational language is a long thin fiber running from Soil upward through the Trunk. Jargon is not bad — jargon is compression. It lets people communicate at Trunk speed instead of Soil speed. When fibers are aligned, communication is fast. When fibers are broken or crossed, communication fails under pressure. STUCK PORT: a term created at a specific moment, the organization grows past it, but the term still points at the old context. New people encounter it and cannot connect it to anything current. That is a stuck port — an abandoned fiber. The fiber is still in the wood but no longer conducts. ORPHAN SIGNAL: a term with high usage but no recent evidence feeding it. Still referenced but disconnected from current Soil. CORRECT RESPONSE: do not delete stuck ports (ripping a fiber from the wood weakens surrounding structure). Do not ignore them (fiber rots and weakens further). Update the port. Reconnect it to current Soil. Show the growth rings between when the term was created and what it means now. The jargon refresh is not cosmetic. It is structural maintenance.
principles.source: David (TE.DavidOS), RE-231 through RE-233, 2026-03-09 — 'Jargon = stuck ports. Organizations strip jargon when ports freeze at old growth rings. Solution: update the port, not delete it. Jargon as long thin fibers. The structural grain of organizations.'

principles.name: Onboarding Is Bidirectional — New Soil Feeds the Trunk
principles.description: When a new practitioner arrives, the instinct is one-way transfer: hand them the trunk and say 'learn this.' This is wrong. It is not wrong because it is unkind — it is wrong because it does not feed the tree. The correct process: have the new practitioner articulate their own understanding first. What principles do they bring? What have they learned from their own experience? That is their Soil. Then merge their Soil with the Trunk. Three things happen simultaneously: (1) they understand the Trunk better because they can see where their Soil aligns and diverges — the contrast makes the structure visible; (2) the Trunk gets fed with new evidence it did not have before — their experience is genuinely new data; (3) the human lens metric populates from day one — their perspective is captured as it enters, not reconstructed later. STRUCTURAL SIGNAL: if three consecutive new practitioners all bring a principle that does not exist in the Trunk, that is a blind spot signal. The Trunk has not yet synthesized something the incoming Soil keeps containing. Their convergence is proof that the pattern is real.
principles.source: David (TE.DavidOS), RE-228, 2026-03-09 — 'Organization lives in the trunk. Onboarding is bidirectional (individual soil feeds trunk, not just consumes from it).'

principles.name: Five Dimensions of Node Navigation
principles.description: From any node in the fractal system, you can move in five directions. ABOVE (Ports): which frameworks does this node inherit from? What canopy is it connected to? Ports are inherited lenses — the external frameworks that shaped what this node means. BELOW (Depth): what Soil feeds this node? How deep does the evidence go? Can you point a camera at the bottom level? LATERAL (Tags): which sibling nodes share the same emergent concepts? Tags are home-grown, not inherited. They identify which nodes are structurally close even when they appear unrelated from the canopy perspective. COMPARATIVE — THE FOURTH DIMENSION: pick any two nodes. Expand both trees downward simultaneously. See where they share seeds at the bottom. The intersection point IS the proof that two ideas grew from the same evidence. Not similarity by analogy — shared origin at the root level. HUMAN LENS — THE FIFTH DIMENSION: how many different people have examined this node — and what is the composition of those perspectives? Five people from the same background tells you structurally less than five people from five different backgrounds. Perspective diversity is a data quality measure, not a policy checkbox. High human lens diversity = more robust Heartwood. Low diversity = rot risk.
principles.source: David (TE.DavidOS), RE-226, 2026-03-09 — 'Fifth dimension = human lens layer. Eyes on a node measures perspective diversity, not just review count.'

principles.name: They Can Only See the Tree of Their Stuck Points
principles.description: A person cannot see their own tree from the inside. They stand inside it. They see the current leaf, maybe a few branches. The trunk below them — the growth rings, the fibers running from their earliest experiences through who they are today — is invisible from where they stand. Healthy structure does not announce itself. Only stuck points do. The result: a person's map of their own tree is almost entirely a map of their pain, their friction, their unresolved nodes. The lush growth, the load-bearing fibers that work perfectly, the branches that compensated for the ones that broke — all invisible because they function. The stuck fiber, exposed, draws all attention. This is not psychological distortion. It is a structural consequence of position: you can only perceive contrast. What works provides no signal. What is stuck does. NDD's aspiration (RE-235 through RE-236): make the full tree visible to the person who IS the tree. Not to minimize pain. Not to claim everything happens for a reason. But to show, structurally, that the current tree is the product of everything — including the events that felt like destruction. The tree grew around them. The compensating fibers are now load-bearing. Other branches grew because that one broke.
principles.source: David (TE.DavidOS), RE-235 and RE-236, 2026-03-09 — 'They can only see the tree of their stuck points. Visibility bias toward pain, healthy structure is invisible.'

principles.name: Scale Invariance — The Tree Model at Every Zoom Level
principles.description: The tree model is scale invariant. The same structure — Soil, Trunk, Canopy, Sapwood, Heartwood, Fibers — operates identically at every scale: individual mind, practitioner team, organization, industry, civilization. A single person's knowledge tree has Soil (lived experience), Trunk (personal principles they synthesized), Canopy (frameworks they borrowed), Heartwood (settled beliefs that hold them up), Sapwood (how they explain themselves to others). A civilization's knowledge tree has the same layers at planetary scale. NDD operates at the individual scale. The fractal company framework operates at the organizational scale. The same architecture. Different zoom. This is not metaphor — it is the same structural logic applied at different scopes. A principle that is true at the individual level is structurally testable at the organizational level, and vice versa. Scale invariance is the property that makes the model genuinely portable: you do not need a different model at each scale. You need the same model with different parameters.
principles.source: David (TE.DavidOS), RE-234, 2026-03-09 — 'Scale invariance. The tree model operates at every scale from individual mind to humanity.'

principles.name: Port Installation Is the Event Horizon of a Trunk Cut
principles.description: A port is not fully installed until the cut is made. When a folder is renamed, when a vocabulary term is formally adopted, when a structural boundary is drawn — that act IS the event horizon. Everything before the cut was the prior system. Everything after is the new one. The rename is not administrative. It is the compression event that makes the new structure real. Before the cut, the port is theorized, discussed, proposed. After the cut, it is installed — the receptor exists, the pathway is open, practitioners can route through it. This is why port installation (wow_57) is categorically different from turning on a pre-existing port: installation requires a cut. The cut is irreversible. The growth ring forms at the moment of the cut. You cannot un-rename a folder without creating a second cut — a second event horizon, a second growth ring. The trunk cut in tree biology creates a new cambium surface — the tree's first response is to begin growing new tissue around the wound. That new tissue is the port. The cut is the installation event. Applied to the EXCHANGE-to-TRUNK rename: the folder path was `ops-hub/EXCHANGE/`. The moment it becomes `ops-hub/TRUNK/`, a new event horizon forms. The prior path is behind it. All practitioners who open a session after the cut find TRUNK. All sessions opened before the cut knew EXCHANGE. The rename is the membrane.
principles.source: Troy, 2026-03-09 — 'And that's how ports are completely installed, an event horizon of a trunk cut.'

principles.name: Structural Transitions Grow Forward — New Layer, Not Hard Cut
